prerequisites: A Microsoft 365 / OneDrive for Business account with files you want to replicate.
An application registered in Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) via App registrations, with the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID recorded.
A client secret generated for the registered app under Certificates & secrets (required for the client-credentials / service key method).
The Microsoft Graph 'Files.Read.All' application permission added to the app, with admin consent granted.
The 'drive_name' of the OneDrive drive to read from, and (optionally) a 'folder_path' to scope replication.
At least one entry configured in 'streams' defining the file type, format, and glob patterns to ingest.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code)
authMethods.summary: Authorize the connector against your Microsoft account through the OAuth login flow, which yields the stored 'credentials.refresh_token' used to obtain Microsoft Graph access tokens on your behalf.

authMethods.name: Client credentials (service key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate the registered Entra ID app directly using 'credentials.client_id', 'credentials.client_secret', 'credentials.tenant_id', and 'credentials.user_principal_name' (the target user's UPN). This relies on the 'Files.Read.All' application permission with admin consent.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal, go to Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) > App registrations > New registration. Create the app and copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID from its Overview page. These map to 'credentials.client_id' and 'credentials.tenant_id'.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: In the app, open Certificates & secrets and create a new client secret. Copy its value immediately and use it as 'credentials.client_secret'. This is required for the client-credentials (service key) authentication method.

setupSteps.title: Grant the Files.Read.All Graph permission
setupSteps.body: Under API permissions, choose Add a permission > Microsoft Graph > Application permissions and add 'Files.Read.All'. Then select Grant admin consent so the connector can read drive files via Microsoft Graph.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: On Airbyte Cloud, use OAuth 2.0 to authorize and store a refresh token ('credentials.refresh_token'). On Open Source / self-hosted, supply the service key fields: 'credentials.client_id', 'credentials.client_secret', 'credentials.tenant_id', and 'credentials.user_principal_name' (the user's UPN).

setupSteps.title: Configure the source fields
setupSteps.body: Set 'drive_name' to your OneDrive drive name (visible in the drive's settings). Optionally set 'folder_path' (leave empty to search all folders) and 'search_scope' (one of 'ACCESSIBLE_DRIVES', 'SHARED_ITEMS', or 'ALL', the default). Optionally set 'start_date' in YYYY-MM-DD format. Define one or more 'streams' specifying file type, format, and glob patterns.

sources.title: Working with files in Microsoft Graph (OneDrive) - Microsoft Learn
